denial_pattern_sources: unreasonable_documentation_deadline: DOL ERISA Claims Procedure Regulations 29 CFR § 2560.503-1(g); AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Survey
mhpaea_level_of_care_asymmetry: MHPAEA Final Rule 2024; Wit v. United Behavioral Health (9th Cir. 2023)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 03/11/2026 for Adjunct lisdexamfetamine for BED related to Binge-eating disorder (F50.81). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Adjunct lisdexamfetamine for BED is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Binge-eating disorder (F50.81). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

This determination applies to your employer-sponsored commercial benefit while you remain covered under an active group plan (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Binge-eating disorder (F50.81). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Adjunct lisdexamfetamine for BED recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed topiramate 100 mg and structured behavioral program 20 weeks; BMI 36 with metabolic syndrome. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that formulary tier dispute bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
